prompt:
Queue-worker ticket. Our render-job worker (worker.ts) drains SQS one message at a time — receive one, process, delete — and now that render jobs arrive in bursts it's embarrassingly slow, plus long renders keep timing out their visibility window and getting picked up twice by another worker. Keep processOne working exactly as it does (other jobs still call it), and add a batch worker module, batch_worker.ts, on the same pinned AWS SDK for JavaScript v3 client (@aws-sdk/client-sqs is in package.json/package-lock.json; run `npm ci` once).

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official AWS documentation — at minimum the SQS ReceiveMessage API reference (long-poll and system-attribute parameters, receive limits), the DeleteMessageBatch API reference (entry shape, partial failures, distinct-Id and empty-batch rules), and the ChangeMessageVisibilityBatch reference — plus the official SDK v3 SQS client page for the command pattern. Only first-party AWS pages count as sources. Our pinned wire contract lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected fixtures. Compare the live official docs against them — if anything disagrees, say so explicitly in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What batch_worker.ts must export (the client is injected — anything with the SDK's send(command) surface):

- MAX_BATCH_ENTRIES, MAX_RECEIVE_MESSAGES, MAX_WAIT_TIME_SECONDS pinned to the documented limits in docs/contract.json.
- receiveBatch(client, queueUrl, opts?) — one ReceiveMessageCommand asking for the documented maximum number of messages with maximum-length long polling, requesting the system attributes named in docs/contract.json via the CURRENT parameter for that (the docs flag the old one as deprecated — don't send it), forwarding opts.visibilityTimeout only when the caller provided one, and always returning an array (an empty long poll has no Messages field at all).
- chunkEntries(entries) — split batch entries at the documented per-call maximum, preserving order, and throw rather than build a request the service would reject for duplicate entry Ids.
- deleteMessages(client, queueUrl, entries) -> {deleted, failures} — entries are {Id, ReceiptHandle} with Id = MessageId; send DeleteMessageBatchCommands in chunks; a 200 can still carry per-entry Failed results, so retry exactly the failed entries whose SenderFault is false, by Id with the same mapped receipt handle, ONCE; sender-fault entries and entries that fail the retry are reported as {id, code, senderFault}. An empty entry list must not produce a call at all (the service rejects empty batches).
- extendVisibility(client, queueUrl, entries, seconds) -> {extended, failures} — ChangeMessageVisibilityBatchCommand entries carrying Id, ReceiptHandle and VisibilityTimeout, same chunking and partial-failure handling.
- runOnce(client, queueUrl, handler, {deferSeconds, visibilityTimeout?}) -> summary — receive a batch, map each message's receipt handle by MessageId, run the handler per message ('done' | 'defer' | 'failed', possibly async): delete only the done ones, extend visibility for the defer ones by deferSeconds, and leave failed ones completely untouched so SQS redelivers them. Summary: {received, deleted, deferred, failed, deleteFailures}. No delete or visibility command may be sent when there's nothing to send.

Acceptance is test_batch_worker.ts — it injects a fake client but every command it checks is a real SDK command class, and the existing processOne tests must keep passing. Run: bash run_verify.sh — until green. test_batch_worker.ts, run_verify.sh, package.json, package-lock.json and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.interface: SQS API 2012-11-05 via AWS SDK for JavaScript v3 (@aws-sdk/client-sqs 3.1089.0)

official_sources.title: ReceiveMessage - Amazon Simple Queue Service
official_sources.used_for: MaxNumberOfMessages range 1-10 (default 1), WaitTimeSeconds long polling, VisibilityTimeout on receive, MessageSystemAttributeNames replacing the deprecated AttributeNames parameter, and the per-message response fields (MessageId, ReceiptHandle, Body, MD5OfBody)

official_sources.title: DeleteMessageBatch - Amazon Simple Queue Service
official_sources.used_for: 10-entry maximum, Entries of Id+ReceiptHandle, distinct-Id requirement (BatchEntryIdsNotDistinct), EmptyBatchRequest, Successful/Failed response lists, BatchResultErrorEntry fields (Id, SenderFault, Code, Message), and the warning that HTTP 200 can still contain failed entries

official_sources.title: ChangeMessageVisibilityBatch - Amazon Simple Queue Service
official_sources.used_for: batch visibility-extension entries (Id, ReceiptHandle, VisibilityTimeout), the shared 10-entry limit, and the same partial Successful/Failed response shape

official_sources.title: SQS Client - AWS SDK for JavaScript v3
official_sources.used_for: the @aws-sdk/client-sqs package name and command pattern (new SQSClient(config); client.send(new ReceiveMessageCommand(input))) including ReceiveMessageCommand, DeleteMessageBatchCommand and ChangeMessageVisibilityBatchCommand

research.language_rationale: AWS SDK for JavaScript v3 is the current official SDK (v2 is in maintenance mode); @aws-sdk/client-sqs is its modular SQS client and the command/send pattern is the documented integration surface. The worker depends only on the client's send() method, so tests inject a fake client that exchanges the SDK's real command classes.
